items.title: Israel’s massive strike was opposite of surgical, Lebanon deputy PM says

children.content: Lebanon's deputy prime minister Tarek Mitri says the government cannot exercise its authority over the country while Israel continues its bombardments and occupation.

gallery.altText: Soldiers standing on top of tanks with a border wall in the distance and a green hill

thumbnail.altText: Soldiers standing on top of tanks with a border wall in the distance and a green hill

items.title: Think of an everyday item — we've tracked how much the price has gone up

children.content: If it feels like everything is getting more expensive, that's because it is. With the rate of inflation rising this week, we've tracked how the price of everyday items has increased.

image.altText: A group of everyday items; fish, light bulb, coffee etc

items.title: Hanson billed taxpayers for flights to fundraiser, to catch Florida jet

children.content: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for two trips that included party fundraisers, discussions with disaffected National Barnaby Joyce, a Gina Rinehart event and return flights to connect the senator to her private jet to Florida, sponsored by her major donor, Mrs Rinehart.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson

items.title: I don't want cold pasta for dinner: Why my appliances aren't cutting it

children.content: While the newest home technology aims to make life easier, when it prioritises aesthetics over usability, some lives are actually made much harder, writes Nas Campanella.

image.altText: A white woman's face is reflected in a shiny black microwave window

avatar.alt: A vision impaired woman with long hair in a blue dress holding a cane

items.title: Coalition's risky Farrer strategy could clear path for One Nation

children.content: Pauline Hanson is now set to tour more regional Coalition-held seats in her "sexy" new private plane, and the Liberal and National parties are helping clear the runway.

image.altText: A mid-shot of Federal Opposition Leader Angus Taylor speaking into microphones during a doorstop outside, in a suit and tie.

avatar.alt: David Speers sits on a couch in front of a yellow screen, smiling at the camera, in a suit and open-necked shirt.

items.title: Has the world’s most powerful cartel met its end?

children.content: A United Arab Emirates decision to pull the plug on its OPEC membership could be the nail in the coffin for an organisation that has struggled to keep its members in line for 66 years.

related.title: More on the global oil crisis:

articles.title: China to help Australia with jet fuel supplies, Wong says

children.content: Australia's foreign minister says the Chinese Government is engaging with Australian businesses on jet fuel supplies as she tours the region.

image.altText: A woman stands in front of the Australian flag.

articles.title: Diesel price surge threatens projects, jobs and household bills

children.content: Civil construction firms warn surging diesel and material costs driven by the global oil crisis will force lay-offs and project delays, with nearly a third considering winding down.

image.altText: Diesel is pumped into a black ute

items.title: ‘It’s a safety issue’: Construction industry faces gambling crisis

image.altText: Men in high-viz and hard hats stand around building supplied chatting.

children.content: New research reveals 89 per cent of construction workers have gambled in the past year — higher than the national average.

items.title: 'Only a little baby': Family of missing 5yo Sharon Granites speaks

children.content: Family members of a missing Alice Springs five-year-old have spoken to the ABC for the first time, pleading for anyone with information that could help find their "energetic" little girl to come forward.

image.altText: Young girl in pink dress does peace sign with fingers

items.title: Insurers among group proposing cheaper premiums for disaster-resistant homes

children.content: As about 1.6 million households experience insurance unaffordability stress, a disparate alliance of stakeholders proposes cheaper premiums for home owners who upgrade their properties to withstand natural disasters.

image.altText: New houses sit on a hillside in a housing estate at Albion Park.

items.title: UK police declare terrorist incident after two Jewish men stabbed in London

children.content: A man has been arrested after two men were stabbed in London in an area with a large Jewish population.

image.altText: A man in high vis and wearing a kippah talks on the phone

items.title: 'Extremely corrosive': Trump called out for remarks aimed at Australia

children.content: US congressman Joe Courtney says the US president's repeated criticism of Australia is "unfair" and causing harm to the US amongst its allies.

image.altText: Trump, wearing a bowtie, points at someone off camera

items.title: King visits 9/11 memorial as he tours New York

children.content: King Charles III and Queen Camilla met survivors and family members of victims, as well as with New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani.

items.title: 'Accident' helps identify giant 'shark-toothed' dinosaur's skull fragment

children.content: Two fossils found in Thailand are named as long-missing pieces of a "shark-toothed" dinosaur after being identified partly by accident.

items.title: APRA Music Awards winners for 2026 announced

children.content: Punk rockers Amyl and The Sniffers have bagged three top gongs at the Australian music industry's APRA awards.

items.title: Gifted, goofy, gone too soon: Jeff Buckley's genius remembered

children.content: The otherworldly singer-guitarist died at 30 and struggled with fame. His guitarist and a new movie honour Buckley's humanity and musical genius.

children.content: Do you know the Union Jack from the Stars and Stripes? And which flags aren't rectangular?

items.title: 'No offence to Tassie': The infamous Sirengate mess 20 years on

children.content: Two decades ago, two teams met at York Park in Launceston. The siren sounded but the match continued and the dreaded word "gate" was added to another footy scandal.

items.title: First report from antisemitism, Bondi royal commission to be released

children.content: The Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion will deliver its first report, two months after it began and less than six months after the Bondi Beach terror attack.

image.altText: Floral tributes at Bondi Beach on day seven after terror attack

items.title: What we know about the fatal plane crash in Adelaide

children.content: Investigations are underway into a light plane crash at an airport in Adelaide's north that claimed two lives and injured 10 others.

image.altText: Smoke among hangars and grass at an airport

items.title: 'Free Palestine' party plans to funnel votes to Victorian conservatives

children.content: Victorian voters are being urged to look out for "misleading" party names, as right-wing activists seek to redirect progressive votes to conservative parties.

image.altText: Avi Yemeni

items.title: Interpreter shortage leaving non-English speakers at risk in court

children.content: As Brisbane courts face delays and backlogs, cases involving people with limited English are being adjourned when interpreters are unavailable — a problem advocates warn is set to worsen.

image.altText: A graphic showing the word "justice" in English and five other languages.

items.title: 'Big lif' is spelling big problems for Vanuatu's pristine rainforests

children.content: A habitat-destroying invasive species is taking over Vanuatu's forests, but one village is fighting back and earning an income for its people at the same time.

gallery.altText: Chief Skip wearing bright orange tshirt cutting down green leaved woody vine.

thumbnail.altText: Chief Skip wearing bright orange tshirt cutting down green leaved woody vine.

items.title: Inside this council's failed campaign to sell the four-day work week

children.content: It wanted to be the first Australian council to usher in a four-day work week. Documents show there was never a standalone business case to back the proposal — but there was a tight grip on messaging.

image.altText: An aerial view of a city during sunset.

items.title: Inflation details may give RBA pause for thought on interest rates

children.content: While inflation remains well above the Reserve Bank's target, there are details in the numbers that may give it reasons to hold off another rate hike in May.

image.altText: A dark-haired woman in glasses casts her gaze downward as she stands at a lectern in forrnt of an RBA backdrop.

children.content: A new research study indicates a majority of regional Australians support the renewable transition despite some protests over planned infrastructure.

items.title: US Supreme Court curbs race-based voting maps in landmark ruling

children.content: The ruling will be seen by civil rights activists as a blow and by the Republican party as a boost ahead of key midterm elections in November.

image.altText: The front facade of the US Supreme Court, a large marble structure with columns.

items.title: Major childcare operator reveals list of 40 centres to close

children.content: Victoria and Western Australia will be affected the most, with 12 centres shutting in each state. NSW, Queensland and South Australia will also have closures.

items.title: Ultra-swimmer sets world record with 55km solo feat in crocodile-filled river

items.title: Docker O'Driscoll reveals he contemplated suicide in emotional post

items.title: What you can do if you are worried about a neighbour's dog

children.content: An unhappy dog doesn't necessarily mean criminal neglect, even if the level of care is below what you would give your own pet.

items.title: Flirtation turns to something much darker in show set at Australian Open

children.content: What starts as a rom-com set at the Australian Open turns into a searing indictment of race relations in Australia in the new show Game. Set. Match.

items.title: Longest-serving WA MP, war hero Bill Grayden, dies at 105

children.content: WA's longest-serving parliamentarian, war hero Bill Grayden, is being remembered for his service to his country and community.

image.altText: Kokoda campaign veteran Bill Grayden at a service in Kings Park.

items.title: Acts of courage during Wieambilla tragedy honoured with police bravery awards

children.content: Police officers and civilians have been honoured for acts of courage during the Wieambilla shootings — which left two officers and a neighbour dead — at a police ceremony in Dalby.
